mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity valuations through higher discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns during rate hiking periods with statistical significance (p < 0.05), or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity repricing within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in 70% of months where unemployment rises by more than 0.4 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand expansion that supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in consumption and housing starts fails to generate positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during post-recession recovery phases signals improving capacity utilization and margin expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day trading window.
failure_condition: Industrial production acceleration yields negative mean 5-day forward returns during recovery periods, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) outside expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad economic expansion that increases corporate profit expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth quarters are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in over 60% of cases, or the correlation coefficient falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly consumer price index increase exceeding 0.5 percent triggers inflation fears that force monetary tightening expectations and compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in 65% of months where CPI inflation exceeds the 0.5 percent monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and real personal consumption expenditures confirm wage-driven demand growth that boosts corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Combined payroll and consumption growth fails to predict positive 20-day forward returns, with a negative mean return observed in triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates during late-cycle expansions signal monetary tightening that compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 returns over a 5-day trading window.
failure_condition: Rate hikes during late-cycle phases produce positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or the signal shows no statistical edge.
